item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Care and love in the Foundation. It is enough for my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple, clean, and comical without going overboard. A neat and inspiring package.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The inspiration for it was actually Lily's 001 proposal; the image of the universe just giving a moment of kindness and beauty as a parting gift to life on Earth, and the Foundation responding by putting aside their ruthlessness just for a moment to take it in, are pretty far outside of my usual headcanon but they're nice to think about. The idea of the Foundation being confronted with a smaller moment of no-strings-attached love and kindness from their normally terrible world, and meeting it halfway by containing the phenomenon but doing something decent themselves in return, stuck with me and this came out of it soon after. It sat in my sandbox for a while, but yesterday seemed like a good day to post it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a fan of this one. It's difficult to make an article both short and interesting but I feel like this has managed it. Good job mate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the premise here—liked it in the draft stage too. It does feel a little more bare-bones than I remember it being, but as a fan of short articles, I admit I'm conflicted over whether that's an issue (and if it is, how much of an issue).

Maybe this would make for a better tale, but this gave me a lot of food for thought and had me still thinking on it for awhile after, despite being so short, so… that's a +1 from me here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to agree with the post above, I feel like you cut this article short. Another issue that I had with this piece is that in the notable incidents table, you say, "Instance repeatedly caused stock cars to manifest outside of hospital…", which reads very weird to me. I think it would make more sense if you referred to them as "subjects" or even "the instance".

item.title: Re: Author post
item.content:encoded:
After you pointed this out, I looked over the article, and found some errors I thought I'd already caught - so those are fixed now, and I'm glad you pointed it out.

This is not my usual subject matter, but I was struck with the idea and wanted to try it. It probably feels short because I don't have much going for me here besides the core image itself; I'm worried that trying to expand on it would just make it worse. This will probably have to stand or fall as it is, but that's okay — can't be afraid of failure if you want to learn.

Thank you for leaving your thoughts!

item.title: Re: Author post
item.content:encoded:
I love the concept, but the execution is a bit poor and it feels too short; I suggest you to edit it.
Anyways, I give you an upvote because this article could be good after a rewrite.

item.title: Not everything has to be gloom and darkness
item.content:encoded:
This is an experiment in writing about/for emotions other than disgust, anxiety, and confusion.

Thanks The Great Hippo and Zyn for looking this over as a draft!

Thank you for reading and voting. No hard feelings if it doesn't work <3
